title: Detective Mystery Craft and Series Variety
summary: Rules for building a fair, clue-driven detective story where diagnosis is earned, treatment is the climax, and series variety is actively rotated.

rules.title: Honor the reader contract
rules.instruction: The story must make the central case question clear early while withholding the diagnosis until the clue chain earns it.
rules.failureMessage: The workbook does not establish a fair central mystery before the diagnosis reveal.

rules.title: Track hidden truth, investigation, and audience interpretation
rules.instruction: Maintain a hidden truth timeline, an investigation timeline, and an audience interpretation timeline so clue timing and reader knowledge are controlled.
rules.failureMessage: The mystery lacks one or more timelines needed for fair clue control.

rules.title: Every major clue has a lifecycle
rules.instruction: Important clues should be planted, misread, pressured, retrieved, reframed, and used in the reveal rather than merely listed.
rules.failureMessage: A clinically important clue is missing a lifecycle step.

rules.title: Reveal by proof chain, not confession
rules.instruction: The diagnosis reveal must cite prior clues and medical mechanism logic before naming the condition.
rules.failureMessage: The diagnosis reveal is unsupported by a prior proof chain.

rules.title: Treatment resolves the case mechanism
rules.instruction: Treatment should be the action climax and should resolve the mechanism uncovered in the reveal.
rules.failureMessage: Treatment is not connected to the revealed mechanism.

rules.title: Close with repair and an opener callback
rules.instruction: The ending must show repair or salvage and close the opener loop.
rules.failureMessage: The story does not include a repair/coda loop back to the opener.

rules.title: Avoid repeated story DNA
rules.instruction: Rotate case type, structure, opening, setting, clue mode, suspense engine, and repair focus against prior story memories.
rules.failureMessage: The story repeats too much prior story DNA without a purposeful variation.

items.label: Central case question appears before diagnostic naming.
Clue ledger includes plant, misread, pressure, retrieve, reframe, and reveal use.
Reveal logic cites prior clues before diagnosis naming.
Treatment action resolves the revealed mechanism.
Wrap-up shows repair and calls back to the opener.

gateCriteria.failureMessage: Story workbook cannot proceed downstream until blocking story craft issues are resolved.
